016package org.consensusj.jsonrpc;
017
018import javax.net.ssl.SSLContext;
019import java.io.IOException;
020import java.lang.reflect.Type;
021import java.net.URI;
022import java.security.NoSuchAlgorithmException;
023import java.util.Base64;
024import java.util.concurrent.CompletableFuture;
025import java.util.concurrent.ExecutionException;
026import java.util.function.Supplier;
027
028/**
029 * Defines the interface for a network-layer implementation of a JSON-RPC client.
030 * @param <T> Type that can be used (in addition to {@link Class}) to declare expected result types for JSON-RPC method calls.
031 */
032public interface JsonRpcTransport<T extends Type> extends AsyncSupport {
033    /**
034     * Get the URI of the remote server
035     * @return URI of remote server
036     */
037    URI getServerURI();
038
039    /**
040     * Send a {@link JsonRpcRequest} for a {@link JsonRpcResponse}
041     * <p>Synchronous subclasses should override this method to prevent {@link CompletableFuture#supplyAsync(Supplier)} from
042     * being called twice when {@link AsyncSupport} is being used. Eventually we'll migrate more of the codebase to native
043     * async, and then we won't have to worry about calling {@code supplyAsync} twice.
044     * @param <R> Type of result object
045     * @param request The request to send
046     * @param responseType The response type expected (used by Jackson for conversion)
047     * @return A JSON RPC Response with `result` of type `R`
048     * @throws IOException network error
049     * @throws JsonRpcStatusException JSON RPC status error
050     */
051    default <R> JsonRpcResponse<R> sendRequestForResponse(JsonRpcRequest request, T responseType) throws IOException, JsonRpcStatusException {
052        return syncGet(sendRequestForResponseAsync(request, responseType));
053    }
054
055    /**
056     * Send a {@link JsonRpcRequest} for a {@link JsonRpcResponse} asynchronously.
057     * @param <R> Type of result object
058     * @param request The request to send
059     * @param responseType The response type expected (used by Jackson for conversion)
060     * @return A future JSON RPC Response with `result` of type `R`
061     */
062    <R> CompletableFuture<JsonRpcResponse<R>> sendRequestForResponseAsync(JsonRpcRequest request, T responseType);
063
064    /**
065     * Synchronously complete a JSON-RPC request by calling {@link CompletableFuture#get()}, unwrapping nested
066     * {@link JsonRpcException} or {@link IOException} from {@link ExecutionException}.
067     * @param future The {@code CompletableFuture} (result of JSON-RPC request) to unwrap
068     * @return A JSON-RPC result
069     * @param <R> The expected result type
070     * @throws IOException If {@link CompletableFuture#get} threw  {@code ExecutionException} caused by {@code IOException}
071     * @throws JsonRpcException If {@link CompletableFuture#get} threw  {@code ExecutionException} caused by {@code JsonRpcException}
072     * @throws RuntimeException If {@link CompletableFuture#get} threw {@link InterruptedException} or other {@link ExecutionException}.
073     */
074    default <R> R syncGet(CompletableFuture<R> future) throws IOException, JsonRpcException {
075        try {
076            return future.get();
077        } catch (InterruptedException ie) {
078            throw new RuntimeException(ie);
079        } catch (ExecutionException ee) {
080            Throwable cause = ee.getCause();
081            if (cause instanceof JsonRpcException) {
082                throw (JsonRpcException) cause;
083            } else if (cause instanceof IOException) {
084                throw (IOException) cause;
085            } else {
086                throw new RuntimeException(ee);
087            }
088        }
089    }
090
091    /**
092     * Encode username password as Base64 for basic authentication
093     * <p>
094     * We're using {@link java.util.Base64}, which requires Android 8.0 or later.
095     *
096     * @param authString An authorization string of the form `username:password`
097     * @return A compliant Base64 encoding of `authString`
098     */
099    static String base64Encode(String authString) {
100        return Base64.getEncoder().encodeToString(authString.getBytes()).trim();
101    }
102
103    /**
104     *
105     * Return the default {@link SSLContext} without declaring a checked exception
106     * @return The default {@code SSLContext}
107     */
108    static SSLContext getDefaultSSLContext() {
109        try {
110            return SSLContext.getDefault();
111        } catch (NoSuchAlgorithmException e) {
112            throw new RuntimeException(e);
113        }
114    }
115}
